transcript.text: This meeting is being recorded.
Absolutely, yeah.
Cool, man, where are you calling from?
Where are you based?
I'm based in Philly.
How about you?
Nice.
I grew up on the East Coast, Boston.
Oh, nice, nice.
Yeah, so you guys got us in the Super Bowl a couple years back.
Oh, yeah, I have a bunch of friends from Philly.
It's actually a really fun city.
I love it.
I'm a big fan.
Obviously, I'm biased, but, yeah, it's a good time here.
Cool, man.
Yeah, we're here in San Francisco.
So the offices, a lot of the teams distributed as well, but most of the heads of the departments are here based in SF.
So kind of perfect timing with all the, you know, the AI craziness, as I'm sure you've heard.
And I know Rachel and your team over there is trying to leverage all the tools and capabilities.
So as much as possible, that's kind of why we stepped in to try to help and why we're talking today.
From you, just so I better understand how to tailor this, but it's going to be super, super casual.
just want to treat this as like, I want to uncover what we don't know and get as much expertise that you have as possible.
Because every step of the way, we've met with experts over at Auth0 to try to make the pieces of our workflow better.
And so we can add your expertise in there, in the healthcare side, but we'd love to hear like a little bit more about you.
Yeah, sure.
So I've been here for about a year and a half at this point in our product marketing org.
So we've got a very matrixed org.
I'm on the verticals and regions team.
So we've got folks that are covering retail, financial services, manufacturing.
During public sector and healthcare.
Each of us are global.
Obviously, like just the nature of the beast, right?
Like we tend to lean towards America, but all of us have a bit of a reach.
But I'm, so healthcare, and that spans payer, provider, life sciences, healthcare IT, as well as the entire Okta suite.
So I do Okta workforce, Okta customer identity, and Auth0 for customer.
Got it.

Well, before being here, I've been in healthcare for the vast majority of my career, whether it's RevCycle, Bearinghouse Solutions, as well as like payment portals and patient portals, different kinds of engagement stuff, or population health analytics.
So that was the hands-on where healthcare are coming into the fold together and now taking all of that and thinking of how to like layer that into the story of identity and security.
Super interesting, man.
Super interesting.
A lot of twists and turns got it to you.
Yeah, I mean, I feel you there.

I think my last three jobs have been, let see, private equity, then starting my own digital marketing business, then a pre-seed incubator accelerator, so venture capital and startups, and then here at GrowthX.
So kind of a crazy ride as well, but all intentional steps, right?
Like all thought through each way, so not random.
So I feel you there.
there.
Cool.
So again, actually just to give you a little context on us, right?
So right now we're helping, we're GrowthX, we're like an AI-powered growth marketing agency.
We do a lot of things.

Our bread and butter is like creating content for websites, but what we're helping Rachel with is more of our custom engagement, which is enabling the Auth0 SDRs with documents and materials to help them sell better.

And so we wanted to start as narrow as possible and where we landed was automatically generating a custom SDR call brief with all of the quick points an SDR would need based on a prospect name and the company that they work for.
And then the second document we have gotten to is a super comprehensive account brief.
So like a high level where an A, you could look at it.

An exec could come in and look at it and it would have everything a seller would need to know about this account in terms of persona, who they're speaking to, how to sell to them, how this company fits into the Auth0 product, what not to say to them, what to say to them.
So that's why we've been doing these expert deep dives is to get as narrow as possible in literally the language and the words that we're using to help these SDRs and these sellers.
And so we started with retail and I think we're talking to better understand the healthcare part of it too.
And I know you oversee the Okta parts as well, not just Auth0.
So that would be some cool insight.
So that's kind of the perspective that I'm coming in from.
And the best way I did it last time, which I think you may know, I know it's a massive company, but Brad Pierce.
That's my good eye.
Yeah, Brad and are the man.
Yeah, yeah.
So I did this exact same one with him.
For retail, like two weeks ago, he was the man.
And so what was helpful is he had a deck that he already just like ran people through or worked off of, like a presentation.
Totally fine if you don't have one, but that was a great starting point for us.
Do you have something similar, like any materials that you usually show or run through?
Yeah, I think I know exactly what he's talking about, and I have a healthcare version.
Give me one second.
No worries, man.

We can always go through the agenda, right, but the questions are a little more boring to go through, where I can just ask them and overlay them over the presentation you're already giving, and we can get in.
I'm going to share my screen real quick.
What he said, did it look kind of like this?
Yeah, how many slides is this?
I think his was massive, if I remember correctly.
Yeah.
It looked like this.
Yeah, it looked like this.
Okay, cool.
I can do, and how much of this do you want to walk?
it
Do me to walk through versus, I know you said you've got some questions as well.
Yeah, so yeah, I think if you want to walk through these high level, I can jump in and ask all the questions we have.
It's more so whatever I'm not unclear on, I can stop you and ask.
I think Brown just like sped through it and, you he had a usual spiel that he gives to everybody.
And then like, you know, the more specific stuff, sometimes we skipped over, but we kind of just went through it relatively quickly.

And then he shot it over after, which was helpful as well, because we can ingest everything and then incorporate Yep, and so what I'll do, I should have, I've got an abridged version for us, because I had to do this for our AEs as well.
So that makes this a lot.
Nice, man.
Cool, let's go slideshow.
So, all right.
So, uh...

What I did there is like, one, obviously, there's just like an example of like recent wins that we've got, but the landscape, the challenges for a number of the organizations, and kind of really the next steps here for where you can find information.
That's one of the things that I take for them, how to engage.
And I'll probably go back and forth between this and the longer version, just because again, there's like interesting tidbits and whatnot that I can pull.
But ultimately, right, and really spanning the entire ecosystem, the goal is to have an integrated and secure digital identity.

And that spans, right, providers, members, their partners, contractors, there's a lot of different things that come into the fold with when it comes to healthcare, and all of them have very like nuanced.
Uh, pain points and needs.
Yep.
Yep.
Um, there's a lot of different types of organizations that we run into.
Uh, I think I have definitions on this.
And for, for context here.
So those one, two, three, four, five, those six, uh, sub verticals.
Um, like what we did for retail was there were like four different personas within retail.
Like it was grocery, food and bath.
Then there was three other like travel and hospitality.
And so we broke down the persona quote unquote into those super niche segments and then tag them with like the actual incoming prospecting company.
So getting this narrow is fantastic for us.
Cause like essentially we would build a, like a master expert document in each one of these fields that would help enable the sell.
So that's kind of like how deep we're trying to go.
So yeah, already off to a great start.
In there, I've got definitions for each of them.
So, so, and some of them are like different.
Even like, for example, like health systems, independent hospitals, provider groups, they're all like your standard provider that you would think of when you think of like a doctor.
It's just the size and scale, right?
So like Mayo Clinic, for example, would be a health system, but like it could be AdvoCare, which is primary care.
I know that's just where I go in Philly, and they've got multiple different locations.
That falls on the provider group.
So the need, the sophistication, the budget, all of that is very different.
Very different.
When it comes to the different organization types.
Which is the biggest, like of these six, like what's the biggest breakdown or the largest percentage within the breakdown of like your actual customers today?
Do you know?
Do you know?
Yes, a number and revenue would be, just call it provider in general, but even more so, like health systems and independent hospitals would be the largest.-hmm.
Payers are definitely significant there as well.
And then Farm and Life Sciences, we've got some of the big ones.
And that's a very interesting use case as well.

I think, right, like, and I was sharing this with Rachel as well, like the nuance and complexity of Okta versus Auth0, at least on the customer identity side, is very, like, tricky here just because, right, like, if you're looking at a pay, or, like, think of Anthem or Aetna, Cigna, these massive companies, they've got all these developer resources.
Same goes for Farm and Life Sciences with your Eli Lilly's of the world, Pfizer.
Globally, or at least, like, in the U.S., you know who these organizations are.
They've got all these development resources.
So a platform like Auth0 makes sense in their direct-to-consumer flows.
If you're talking, like, a even midsize or some of the larger...
Health systems in the U.S., not all of them have a dev team.
So having a platform that is meant to be like developer first and you build on top of it, like Auth0 doesn't always make sense.
Whereas Okta customer identity, it's more of a true like plug and play.
You press a toggle and your identity solution is on.
It's a no code option.
But that said, right, like, I don't know, there's a lot of different nuance that goes into that as well.
Because even if they don't have a ton of dev resources, they might be looking to tie together a patient portal, educational resources, outreach, all of that stuff, donors.
And then having something that can unify all that in a streamlined way like Auth0 is where, you know, it's just it's a lot of almost like decision tree.
But that said.
What we also know about the space is, one, there's a massive opportunity.
This is a global figure, 800,000 healthcare companies.
That's a very blanketed term because, as we just said, there's a lot of different types of healthcare companies with a significant presence in the Fortune 500.
M&A is a huge sticking point.
As I just mentioned, unifying different digital portfolios as well as your workforce.
If you have two different active directories, there's going to be the need to tie all of that together somehow.
That's where Okta Workforce can come into the play.
The IT market's in a boom right now, not just across technology, but AI.
I want to say it's the largest adopter of AI across all of the various industry segments.
And then the TAM is really just more of Okta internal figure.
This is more for why it matters at Okta, so significant presence in terms of, like, some of our white whale accounts are coming from the healthcare space.
And then there's obviously a bunch of different unique attributes here as well.
One thing that I think is very interesting to call out that's, like, in addition to this, you mentioned, like, the different segments you guys touched on in retail.
So the interesting part in healthcare is, like, how you refer to the end user in a lot of cases, right?
So anytime it's a provider organization, it's patient rather than customer.
Anytime you're speaking to a payer, it's member rather than patient or customer.
And then when you're speaking to, like, life sciences, that's where things get interesting because technically speaking, they're oftentimes a patient, right?
It's like clinical trials.
But then, like, some...
Some orgs view them as customers as well.

So like Eli Lilly, for example, where they've got, you know, like a direct-to-consumer, like where they're pushing GLP-1s and various telehealth stuff, they're viewing them as customers who just also happen to be patients.
So there's a lot of nuance there.

On the customer identity side, there's also the fact that like HIPAA is going to be involved in pretty much some way, shape, or form because that interaction of like signing on or whether it's consent management or any other kind of like data input there is like touching some form of PHI.
The other piece that makes things complicated is like, it's historically been an industry that's slow to move.
There's a lot of siloed architecture, a lot of on-prem technology as well.
Although I will say in the last couple of years, we have seen a significant shift.
And to the cloud, the last piece of things makes things interesting is sometimes, and it's not like a guaranteed thing, but we might run into like a purchasing organization as well.
So I list out like Vizient, Kauffman Hall, these digital transformation firms that effectively are acting like a supply chain management to like vet vendors sometimes.
That's like a market dynamic that we've run into.
Interesting.
Yeah.
So when you, so when you, so like when you're selling to someone before they say yes, they loop in this consultant sometimes to.
It's not a sure thing.
We've had plenty of success not going that route.
It's, I just have heard anecdotally, there are.
Certain organizations that rely on, Vizient's a good example, where they've got like a network, almost a marketplace of like their preferred partners that they operate with.
Same with like, same as with like a Deloitte, like a GSI, or where, hey, you're coming to us to help, you know, build your tech stack out for you or with you.
So, here's who we work with today and where we've had success in the past.
Makes sense.
Scrolling through here, I have a bunch of different like personas.
I don't know if you, for us, they're pretty standardized across like the ecosystem.
By that, you mean all of the different verticals or what?
Yeah, yep.
In terms of like, obviously the message that we hit on is a little bit different, but like, who we're
We're actually speaking to is generally speaking going to be somewhat similar.
That said, though, right, like from a customer identity standpoint, the way that we typically will lean in on this is like identity powered customer journeys or patient journeys.
Realistically, this is, again, just me keeping this in the Okta jargon, voting for speed and scale and securing the digital front door.
I'll share like some of the official messaging documents as well on this.
On the workforce side, we get into protecting against cyber threats because obviously another interesting tidbit is the frequency, the end scale of cyber attacks that happen in the healthcare space.
It's nearly 11 million per breach and it's like twice as much for healthcare as it is like the global average for like toxic breaches.
So it's something that's always, like, top of mind.
There's obviously reputational damage that comes with that.
There's operational impact, financial impact, obviously, like any kind of HIPAA fine.
I always liken back to, like, the change healthcare breach a couple years ago.
It's like nobody wants to be the next change.
Yeah, That part obviously applies more to the clinical workforce, but there's also an entire, like, back office.
Like provisioning, deprovisioning, governing access to who can use what and when that comes into the fold here.
I think an interesting piece as well is like the different hats from like a clinical standpoint that folks may have to wear, right?

Like if you're an academic medical center, at one point in the day, you might be a professor versus, you know, later on or the next day, you are now like in a clinical setting operating as a clinician.
So like the different level of access that you're going to need in terms of like resources and whatnot can greatly vary.
So that's just another piece of the puzzle to keep in mind.
Definitely.
Any, so like from your standpoint, like with the questions, what are some of the other things that are like most, like top of mind that you'd like to get an understanding of?
Just because, again, like I know I'll share all of this.
It's a lot of different angles that we could press on.
Yeah, so I think, first of all, like I wanted to see where you would go because like where people go is usually what they talk about the most, which are the most important things.
That's usually what correlates.
But yeah, for us, what we're looking for is what you already covered or touched on, which is messaging, right?
And so I think we'll get the rest of the deck and the rest of the messaging documents where we can go deeper there.
So nothing more needed there.
The second is like who we're actually speaking to, who the SDRs are actually selling to.
And I know we got some of that and the breakdown of the six, but then, yeah, there's the keeperstone of breakdown here.
I don't know if there's anything high level here that you want to share.
Every now and then.
So what we'll see time and time again is there's oftentimes like a separation of, especially at the larger organization, of who.
Owning customer identity versus workforce.
Oftentimes, it's like CIO, CISO that are owning the workforce side of the house.
And then it could be either digital in large scale.
it's like they have their own patient experience officer.
They might be there in the fold as well.
And it's interesting because we've run into clients where in both cases, it's identity, but it's almost as if they're completely separate businesses.
They don't talk to each other whatsoever in terms of purchasing.
So one of us can be our biggest advocate and the other one can be like, who?
What?
I'm not using that.
If you guys could walk down the hall and have a conversation, that would make this a lot easier.
So, but yeah, that's, I'd say like generally speaking.
Like we run into IT pretty regularly on multiple fronts.
Workforce, obviously, if they have developers or an engineering team or product, whatever it may be, that's all present on the customer identity side.
Got it.
That makes a ton of sense.
And then, so what are like the biggest pain points that you're solving for like each one of these personas or more general?
Like what are the top pain points that you're seeing?
Like the problems that Okta for some or Auth0 for like the other three that you mentioned, what are the big ones they're solving?
So Auth0, right, time to value is a big one.
That and there's the extensibility of the platform, right?
Like you can make it how you want effectively.
And then there's the...
The ability to pull in all these different data sources to paint more of like a 360 of your patient or member, right?
So doing progressive profiling to get a better profile on the patient, whether it's you're connecting to a CDP to have more data on them and understand their preferences.
And then all of this combined with security as well.

I'll also add pieces in there from a workforce standpoint that can manage some consent management, which obviously like with HIPAA and delegating access to like a parent or whatnot or a child is going to be critical in health care.
That alongside, right, like things like smart on fire capability or standard support as well as FGA, we find that to be a big one.
So fine-grained authorization.
Giving folks access to certain resources is something that we run into regularly on the customer side.
If we look at workforce, there's the, obviously, security is a big one.
That's actually one of the stronger ones that we lean on.
I mentioned, like, cost of data breaches, having solutions that can automate, like, threat detection and response is something that is certainly top of mind.

There's also this unified platform identity security fabric that we talk about where it's pulling all of these pieces across the ecosystem together and being able manage that with a single source of identity, right?

So whether you're requiring to federate from Okta into multiple different apps, whether it's, you know, you just bought or you're merging with a different health system and now you have your store of what.
Active Directory, they have theirs.

You plug into Okta, you can tie all of that together, and it really tightens down your posture so that as you're trying to get through or towards a zero-trust architecture, there's an actual pathway there.
That plus, there's the, in terms of also, I guess, extensibility here, right, is the Okta integration network as well.
So, I think we've got something like 8,000 partners that we work with, right, that are, by design, it's like you don't have to do any custom code.
All you have to do is connect the dots, and we do all of the upkeep on our end from an API access standpoint.

Also, I guess, like, the full solution kind of spin of it, too, is, like, beyond just identity and access management, there is a governance component, privilege access, so you're protecting your private keys and your architecture, all of that.
Um, and just all of it being done through like an orchestration layer as well.
So they're automating as much of these tasks as possible.
So it no longer becomes a manual task that's, you know, driving you crazy and having more.
It's a always on engine that can just kind of run.
I like that.
I like that, man.
Um, this is really, really helpful.
I mean, I feel like I could talk for another hour about this, but, um, we can get, you know, the deck and any of the materials that you can send over would be awesome.
Um, but like what those materials didn't have is all the info you gave me.
It's just like your expertise, you adding color to all of these was really, really helpful.
And like, it's going to get us to a point where we're going to be able to get like really, really, really specific and niche with the, like the documents that we're creating.
Yeah, I'm happy to share all of this over.
there's also some other decks I can share, like market research that I've done with like one of our partners.
Um,
They're healthcare specific and they have an IAM branch.
So like there's already insights that we can pull into this as well.
So I've got a laundry list of things I can send you.
Fantastic.
Appreciate it, Nick.
Yeah, would love that over Slack or however is easiest for you works with us.
And yeah, this is really helpful.
can keep you updated on the progress we're making because we're trying to make all your sellers' lives easier.
And then hopefully that trickles upstream to everyone who works with them or is involved with, honestly, the entire organization.
So I'll keep you updated on the progress.
Yeah, I talked to Rachel and was very pumped about this.
So I'm looking forward to it.
Let's go, man.
Let's go.
Hopefully we talk again soon, but for now, I really appreciate it.
Of course.
And thanks for taking the time today.
Yes, sir.
Have a good one.
You too.

default_summary.markdown_formatted:
## Meeting Purpose

[Gather expert insights on Okta's healthcare vertical for creating tailored SDR and sales enablement materials.](https://fathom.video/share/jSCVxUQzVFAXq7q3YSNa2Hf7WdAf1_yG?tab=summary&timestamp=0.0)

## Key Takeaways

  - [Healthcare vertical spans 6 sub-segments: health systems, independent hospitals, provider groups, payers, pharma/life sciences, healthcare IT](https://fathom.video/share/jSCVxUQzVFAXq7q3YSNa2Hf7WdAf1_yG?tab=summary&timestamp=6.0)
  - [Nuanced messaging needed (e.g., "patient" for providers, "member" for payers)](https://fathom.video/share/jSCVxUQzVFAXq7q3YSNa2Hf7WdAf1_yG?tab=summary&timestamp=71.0)
  - [Auth0 suits dev-heavy orgs (e.g., large payers, pharma); Okta CIAM better for smaller providers](https://fathom.video/share/jSCVxUQzVFAXq7q3YSNa2Hf7WdAf1_yG?tab=summary&timestamp=711.0)
  - [Key pain points: security/cyber threats, unifying disparate systems, time-to-value, extensibility](https://fathom.video/share/jSCVxUQzVFAXq7q3YSNa2Hf7WdAf1_yG?tab=summary&timestamp=744.0)

## Topics

### Healthcare Landscape Overview

  - [800,000+ global healthcare companies, significant Fortune 500 presence](https://fathom.video/share/jSCVxUQzVFAXq7q3YSNa2Hf7WdAf1_yG?tab=summary&timestamp=780.0)
  - [$11M avg. cost per data breach (2x global average)](https://fathom.video/share/jSCVxUQzVFAXq7q3YSNa2Hf7WdAf1_yG?tab=summary&timestamp=1129.0)
  - [Historically slow to adopt new tech, but recent shift to cloud](https://fathom.video/share/jSCVxUQzVFAXq7q3YSNa2Hf7WdAf1_yG?tab=summary&timestamp=956.0)
  - [Unique challenges: HIPAA compliance, siloed architecture, on-prem legacy systems](https://fathom.video/share/jSCVxUQzVFAXq7q3YSNa2Hf7WdAf1_yG?tab=summary&timestamp=949.0)
  - [Some orgs use purchasing consultants (e.g., Vizient, Kauffman Hall) to vet vendors](https://fathom.video/share/jSCVxUQzVFAXq7q3YSNa2Hf7WdAf1_yG?tab=summary&timestamp=1020.0)

### Sub-Vertical Breakdown

  - [Providers (largest segment): health systems, independent hospitals, provider groups](https://fathom.video/share/jSCVxUQzVFAXq7q3YSNa2Hf7WdAf1_yG?tab=summary&timestamp=596.0)
  - [Payers: significant presence (e.g., Anthem, Aetna, Cigna)](https://fathom.video/share/jSCVxUQzVFAXq7q3YSNa2Hf7WdAf1_yG?tab=summary&timestamp=660.0)
  - [Pharma/Life Sciences: dev-heavy orgs (e.g., Eli Lilly, Pfizer)](https://fathom.video/share/jSCVxUQzVFAXq7q3YSNa2Hf7WdAf1_yG?tab=summary&timestamp=699.0)
  - [Healthcare IT: growing segment with increasing AI adoption](https://fathom.video/share/jSCVxUQzVFAXq7q3YSNa2Hf7WdAf1_yG?tab=summary&timestamp=828.0)

### Product Fit: Okta vs. Auth0

  - [Auth0: Suits dev-heavy orgs (large payers, pharma) for custom implementations](https://fathom.video/share/jSCVxUQzVFAXq7q3YSNa2Hf7WdAf1_yG?tab=summary&timestamp=1426.0)
  - [Okta CIAM: Better for smaller providers, "plug-and-play" solution](https://fathom.video/share/jSCVxUQzVFAXq7q3YSNa2Hf7WdAf1_yG?tab=summary&timestamp=740.0)
  - [Decision factors: in-house dev resources, desired customization, existing tech stack](https://fathom.video/share/jSCVxUQzVFAXq7q3YSNa2Hf7WdAf1_yG?tab=summary&timestamp=1434.0)

### Key Personas and Buying Centers

  - [Often separate owners for customer identity vs. workforce solutions](https://fathom.video/share/jSCVxUQzVFAXq7q3YSNa2Hf7WdAf1_yG?tab=summary&timestamp=1320.0)
  - [Customer identity: Digital teams, Patient Experience Officers](https://fathom.video/share/jSCVxUQzVFAXq7q3YSNa2Hf7WdAf1_yG?tab=summary&timestamp=1335.0)
  - [Workforce: CIO, CISO](https://fathom.video/share/jSCVxUQzVFAXq7q3YSNa2Hf7WdAf1_yG?tab=summary&timestamp=1324.0)
  - [Can be siloed, requiring separate sales approaches](https://fathom.video/share/jSCVxUQzVFAXq7q3YSNa2Hf7WdAf1_yG?tab=summary&timestamp=1354.0)

### Pain Points and Solutions

  - [Auth0:](https://fathom.video/share/jSCVxUQzVFAXq7q3YSNa2Hf7WdAf1_yG?tab=summary&timestamp=1426.0)
      - [Time-to-value, extensibility](https://fathom.video/share/jSCVxUQzVFAXq7q3YSNa2Hf7WdAf1_yG?tab=summary&timestamp=1431.0)
      - [360° patient/member view (progressive profiling, CDP integration)](https://fathom.video/share/jSCVxUQzVFAXq7q3YSNa2Hf7WdAf1_yG?tab=summary&timestamp=1440.0)
      - [Consent management for HIPAA compliance](https://fathom.video/share/jSCVxUQzVFAXq7q3YSNa2Hf7WdAf1_yG?tab=summary&timestamp=1468.0)
      - [Smart on FHIR capability, fine-grained authorization (FGA)](https://fathom.video/share/jSCVxUQzVFAXq7q3YSNa2Hf7WdAf1_yG?tab=summary&timestamp=1487.0)
  - [Okta Workforce:](https://fathom.video/share/jSCVxUQzVFAXq7q3YSNa2Hf7WdAf1_yG?tab=summary&timestamp=1511.0)
      - [Security (automated threat detection/response)](https://fathom.video/share/jSCVxUQzVFAXq7q3YSNa2Hf7WdAf1_yG?tab=summary&timestamp=1518.0)
      - [Unified identity security fabric](https://fathom.video/share/jSCVxUQzVFAXq7q3YSNa2Hf7WdAf1_yG?tab=summary&timestamp=1534.0)
      - [Zero-trust architecture enablement](https://fathom.video/share/jSCVxUQzVFAXq7q3YSNa2Hf7WdAf1_yG?tab=summary&timestamp=1546.0)
      - [Okta Integration Network (8,000+ pre-built integrations)](https://fathom.video/share/jSCVxUQzVFAXq7q3YSNa2Hf7WdAf1_yG?tab=summary&timestamp=1574.0)
